summary: A rectangular multi-story institutional-style building with a stepped red-tiled gabled roof, four regular floors of window bands, horizontal stone/wood banding, and two tall corner towers with overhanging platform caps. The structure sits on a low white-block border/curbstone and is surrounded by cleared ground.

shape.overall_form: Rectangular main block with two tall narrow corner towers and chamfered front corner; long façade has horizontal repeating bands and continuous narrow windows.
shape.symmetry: approximate bilateral symmetry along the long axis (towers at opposite ends, repetitive floor bands)

materials.name: quartz_white
materials.role: low border/curbstone and vertical entrance posts

elements: continuous narrow horizontal windows on each floor (repeated bands)
two tall corner towers with small square windows and overhanging capped platforms
stepped layered red-tile gabled roof with white eaves
white block low perimeter border and vertical white entrance posts
stonebrick vertical corner trims and banding between floors
rebuild_hints: Main rectangular block: four repeating floor bands; each floor band has a strip of narrow windows set in sandstone with a wooden accent strip above and stonebrick trim between floors.
Roofs: create a symmetrical stepped gable roof using red brick stairs (or similar) with white eaves (quartz or slab) forming a visible lip.

Towers: place two tall 3-4 block wide towers at opposite ends, with darker stonebrick vertical edges and a lighter sandstone face; add small square windows vertically spaced; top with an overhanging flat platform of layered slabs and a small cap block (glowstone?)
Base: surround the building with a single-layer white block curb spaced one block from walls; ground is flattened dirt/terracotta.
Chamfer the front-right corner by stepping the wall inward on each floor to match the visible angled corner profile.
uncertainties: Exact block IDs: sandstone is inferred for the pale yellow walls but could be smooth_sandstone or cut_sandstone; I labeled as 'sandstone'.
Roof material identified as 'brick_tile' (looks like red brick or terracotta stairs); exact stair/block type uncertain.
Wood accent could be oak planks or birch; labeled generically as wood_planks.
Height and footprint are estimated from perspective; actual interior floor heights and exact tower spacing are approximate.
